title: System intent satisfaction: context preservation
scope: Governing intent: Preserve only the context that is expensive to rediscover and necessary for safe continuation.
goal: Assess whether current package surfaces satisfy the context-preservation intent.
non_goals: Do not open a new remediation lane from this review alone.
Do not treat every useful fact as Memory-worthy.

review_mode.review question: Does the current package preserve costly continuation context without over-preserving one-off execution detail?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: startup durable_intent report, system intent mirror, README, memory index, planning review guidance

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section durable_intent --format json; rg intent/context/proof/handoff terms across README, docs, and .agentic-workspace surfaces

findings.title: Satisfaction is high, with review-residue pressure as the main watchpoint
findings.summary: The package now has clear boundaries for active Planning, durable Memory, compact startup routing, and closeout distillation, which directly supports preserving only costly continuation context.
findings.evidence: README frames the package around context that disappears between sessions; Memory index says planning/status owns active intent; module-map says completed execplans should be distilled and removed; review README says acted-upon review artifacts should shrink, stub, or delete.
findings.risk if unchanged: If review artifacts keep accumulating after promotion, they can become a second archive of context that is no longer necessary for continuation.
findings.suggested action: Keep using closeout/review retention checks as the main guard; promote a cleanup issue only if acted-upon reviews repeatedly remain full-size.
findings.promotion target: planning roadmap only if repeated stale-review residue is observed
findings.promotion trigger: A future reconcile/review pass finds multiple acted-upon reviews that still carry reconstructable source context.

recommendation.defer: Watch review artifact retention during future closeout and reconcile passes.
recommendation.dismiss: No immediate implementation issue is justified by this review alone.

retention.trigger: review-residue concern is promoted, dismissed, or superseded by a stronger reconcile signal
retention.proof surface: planning reconcile output, review retention checks, Memory routing docs

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section durable_intent --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-08: Review created and filled from durable-intent doctrine pass.
